theme: A librarian leaves deliberate holes on the shelves before a donation arrives
title: Gap-Seeded Shelf — offline slack placement for a known insert-and-scan future
strategies: trivial: pack all keys flush at the front, gaps at the tail -> reproduces the checker baseline (~0.1); scans are cheap but every insert shifts a long block to the far gap

greedy: textbook packed-memory-array reflex -- spread keys uniformly over the whole capacity so inserts always have a nearby gap; blind to the visible trace, it dilutes EVERY scan and wastes slack on cold regions that never receive an insert (the amortized-worst-case hedge is the trap)

strong: treat gap placement as a TRANSPORT problem -- count how many future inserts land in each inter-key interval and carve a reservoir there sized to that demand, leaving scan-heavy cold intervals packed solid and parking spare capacity far to the right; inserts land near-free while dense regions keep scans cheap
alternatives: demand-weighted density gradient, occasional targeted rebuilds before a hotspot re-heats, or a cost-model DP over per-interval slack vs scan traffic
